rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed electrochemical C-H activation/annulation of 8-aminoquinoline benzamides with allenylphosphine oxides to form isoquinolinone-type products
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Co(II) to Co(III) at the graphite felt anode; cathodic H2 evolution at Pt; the high-valent Co species mediates C-H metalation/migratory insertion/C-N bond formation

key_intermediates.name: Cobalt(III) complex bearing chiral oxazoline ligand L3, benzamide-derived cyclometalated aryl, and 4-methoxypyridine coordinated (compound 45)
Co(II) precatalyst Co(OAc)2·4H2O ligated by L3
Cyclometalated Co(III)-aryl after concerted metalation-deprotonation (CMD) of benzamide C-H

electron_flow: The GF anode oxidizes the Co(II)/benzamide complex to a Co(III) species, which undergoes carboxylate-assisted (NaOPiv) C-H metalation of the benzamide; migratory insertion of the allene followed by C-N reductive elimination yields the product, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: C-H metalation is partially rate-influencing but not fully rate-limiting (small KIE ~1.2 suggests C-H cleavage is not solely rate-determining)

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-benzamide 1 (intermolecular competition with non-deuterated 1)
probe_reagents.observation: PH/PD ≈ 1.2 from 1H NMR of product, indicating C-H cleavage is not the rate-determining step

probe_reagents.name: 4-methoxypyridine (as donor ligand to trap Co(III) intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric electrolysis (1.0 equiv Co, 1.0 equiv L3, 1.0 mA, 12 h) furnished isolable Co(III) complex 45 in 12% yield, supporting a Co(III) cyclometalated intermediate

notes: Only two mechanistic probes shown on this page (KIE and isolation of a Co(III) intermediate via stoichiometric electrolysis). The small KIE (~1.2) argues against C-H activation being rate-determining; the isolated Co(III) complex 45 supports an inner-sphere electrocatalytic Co(II)/Co(III) cycle with carboxylate-assisted CMD.
